meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build advanced user onboarding to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. A masterclass case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming 'no project ideas' starts by building complex infrastructure that solves a universal SaaS pain point: user onboarding. By engineering a high-conversion, interactive onboarding flow, you demonstrate architectural maturity. Use Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for iterative refinement to turn a simple concept into a professional-grade portfolio asset.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic, mocking cadence. You have the technical skills, but the 'what' remains elusive. Most students fall into the trap of cloning to-do lists, but the industry doesn't need another generic task manager—it needs sophisticated user experiences that drive retention.

True engineering mastery isn't found in reinventing the wheel, but in perfecting the gateway to value. By shifting your focus from 'what app to build' to 'how to onboard users into a complex workflow,' you stop chasing ideas and start building the foundation of every successful SaaS platform. This is how you transform a student project into a professional-grade case study.

steps.title: Architecting the State Machine
steps.description: Define the onboarding states (Welcome, Preference Gathering, Value Prop, Dashboard Activation) to ensure a modular, scalable architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust React state machine architecture for a multi-step user onboarding flow using XState, including types for transition guards and context persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new directory /onboarding and scaffold the state machine structure based on the provided Antigravity spec.'

steps.title: Engineering Dynamic Preference Inputs
steps.description: Create a data-driven form system that adapts based on user selections to provide a personalized 'aha!' moment immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dynamic form schema generator that updates onboarding UI components based on user persona selection (e.g., Developer vs. Designer).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the dynamic form component using the generated schema and integrate it into the onboarding flow.'

steps.title: Implementing Contextual Progress Tracking
steps.description: Use progress bars and milestone markers to reduce cognitive load and increase user completion rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a progress tracking hook that calculates completion percentage based on active step indices and persisted user context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add the progress tracker hook to the onboarding layout component and ensure state persistence in localStorage.'

steps.title: Integrating AI-Driven Personalization
steps.description: Use LLM-injected context to generate personalized welcome messages based on the gathered user preferences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that takes onboarding persona data and returns a personalized welcome string using a clean, professional tone.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the personalization utility into the final step of the onboarding sequence.'

steps.title: Optimizing for Conversion Metrics
steps.description: Instrument the flow with event tracking to measure where users drop off, allowing for data-backed iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level event tracking wrapper for the onboarding flow that logs step transitions and form completion data to a mock analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap all state transitions with the new analytics tracker and verify logs in the console.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a decoupled micro-frontend; it allows you to plug it into any future project.
Use 'optimistic UI' updates for form submissions to make the onboarding feel instantaneous, even with backend latency.
Never ask for more than three inputs before providing a tangible value-add to the user.
Implement 'exit intent' triggers in your onboarding to capture feedback if a user tries to skip.
Treat onboarding as a product feature, not a utility—the copy should be as polished as your landing page.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, invite 5 peers to test it, and document the conversion metrics to showcase in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have a high-value, functional onboarding module that elevates any SaaS project. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the features that companies actually pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
